title: Glass Anatomy Cuts to the Bone on Severance Songs
subtitle: The Rotterdam trio trades maximalism for negative space and finds its sharpest record yet
metaDescription: Review of Severance Songs by Glass Anatomy.
heroImageAlt: Flat illustration of a lone figure seated at a table in warm light

chapters.title: Less signal, more nerve
chapters.bodyHtml:
Where 2024's Full Bloom Static buried every hook under three synth layers, Severance Songs strips the band to drums, one detuned guitar, and Lena Vos's voice recorded so close you hear the room. The restraint reads as confidence, and the confidence is earned.

PHONOGRAPH rating

The record's spine is sequencing: three ballads in the middle stretch that would sag on a lesser album instead build pressure for the title track's release. Nothing here begs for playlist placement, which is precisely why it will get it.

keyPoints.text: Sparse arrangements foreground the strongest vocal work of the band's run.
Mid-album ballad sequence is structural, not filler.
Best entry point for new listeners despite being a fourth album.
